data.content_ref: inline:{"claim":"Emergent coastal alliances instituted a systematic naval blockade of Eastern Mediterranean trade lanes, choking the resource inflow to palace economies.","mechanism":"Maritime coalition economic warfare that forced loss of imports and destabilised elite redistribution.","scope":{"covers":["Mycenaean Greece","Hittite Anatolia","Ugarit","Levantine city‑states"],"excludes":["Egypt"]},"forbidden":[{"case":"If Egyptian and Hittite annals from the period record no reference to any large‑scale maritime embargo despite their detailed military chronicles","eval":"rubric:std-hist"}],"prose_notes":"Focuses on inscriptional evidence of shipwreck layers and disrupted cargo typologies, moving beyond the classic Sea‑Peoples raid narrative."}
